fields: A framework for developing applications powered by large language models (LLMs).
A data framework for LLM-based applications to ingest, structure, and access private or domain-specific data.
An open-source NLP framework by deepset to build search systems and LLM applications.
An experimental open-source application showcasing the capabilities of the GPT-4 language model to act autonomously.
A framework for orchestrating role-playing, autonomous AI agents to work together on complex tasks.
A library for building stateful, multi-actor applications with LLMs, built on top of LangChain.
A framework for programming—rather than prompting—language models, focusing on declarative modules and optimizers.
An open-source SDK by Microsoft that lets you easily combine AI services with conventional programming languages.
A simplified version of an autonomous AI agent that uses LLMs to create, prioritize, and execute tasks.
A guidance language for controlling large language models more effectively than traditional prompting.
A library for structured text generation that ensures LLM outputs follow a specific schema.
A programming language designed for AI development that combines the syntax of Python with the performance of C.
A high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for LLMs.
A toolkit by Hugging Face for deploying and serving Large Language Models.
An open-source, locally running implementation of OpenAI's Code Interpreter.
A standard for communication between AI agents and the platforms that run them.
An enterprise-grade Python framework for building AI applications with LLMs.
A framework for building AI Assistants with memory, knowledge, and tools.
A code-first agent framework for seamless planning and execution of data analytics tasks.
A multi-agent framework that assigns different roles to GPTs to form a collaborative software entity.
OpenAI's next-generation model released in late 2025, featuring vastly improved multimodal reasoning, generation, and agency.
Anthropic's next-generation model, released in early 2026, setting new industry benchmarks for AI safety, long-context reasoning, and natural conversation.
Google's flagship model released in 2025, featuring a 4 million token context window and significantly improved logical reasoning and agentic capabilities.
Meta's powerful and efficient open-source model released in 2025, featuring a new, more efficient architecture and setting a new standard for open-source LLMs.
Mistral AI's premier closed-source model designed for complex multilingual reasoning tasks.
A strong Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) language model known for high efficiency and performance.
xAI's frontier model with improved reasoning capabilities and a 128k context window.
OpenAI's text-to-video model capable of generating realistic and imaginative scenes.
A state-of-the-art text-to-image model family developed by Black Forest Labs.
Stability AI's most capable text-to-image model using a Diffusion Transformer architecture.
Cohere's scalable model optimized for enterprise-grade RAG and tool use.
OpenAI's latest image generation model that understands significantly more nuance and detail.
An open-source automatic speech recognition (ASR) system trained by OpenAI.
AI21 Labs' hybrid SSM-Transformer model architecture.
Snowflake's open, enterprise-grade MoE model designed for efficient inference.
Microsoft's lightweight, 3.8B parameter model that outperforms models twice its size.
Databricks' open, general-purpose LLM that set a new state-of-the-art for open models.
A family of lightweight, state-of-the-art open models built from the same research as Gemini.
Alibaba Cloud's latest generation of large language models and large multimodal models.
LPU (Language Processing Unit) Inference Engine specifically designed for LLMs to achieve record speeds.
A technique that grants LLMs access to specific, up-to-date information through data retrieval.
A paradigm where LLMs generate reasoning traces and task-specific actions in an interleaved manner.
A prompting technique that encourages the model to generate intermediate steps before the final answer.
A framework that allows LLMs to explore multiple reasoning paths and self-evaluate choices.
An agent pattern where the model critiques its own output to improve subsequent iterations.
The ability of an LLM to generate structured output that can be used to invoke external APIs or functions.
A design where multiple specialized agents collaborate or compete to solve complex tasks.
Iterative loops where AI agents plan, execute, and refine their work autonomously.
Techniques like RAG or specialized architectures to handle and reason over massive amounts of input data.
The process of refining and optimizing inputs to LLMs to achieve desired outputs.
Providing a few examples in the prompt to guide the model's behavior and output format.
Asking the model to perform a task without providing any prior examples.
A technique that samples multiple reasoning paths and selects the most consistent answer.
A strategy where the model first creates a plan and then executes it step-by-step.
A framework that models LLM reasoning as a non-linear graph for more complex problem-solving.
Providing agents with short-term and long-term memory to maintain context over time.
A process where the model continuously improves a draft based on feedback or new information.
A method to speed up LLM generation by first creating a skeleton and then filling in details in parallel.
A technique where the model generates a draft, identifies potential errors, and verifies them.
A strategy that breaks down a complex problem into simpler sub-problems and solves them sequentially.
The AI research and deployment company behind GPT-4, DALL-E, and Sora.
An AI safety and research company, creator of the Claude family of models.
Google's AI research lab responsible for Gemini, AlphaGo, and AlphaFold.
Meta's research division focused on open science and the Llama model family.
The leading provider of GPU hardware and software platforms (CUDA) for AI training and inference.
A suite of AI services and tools provided by Microsoft's cloud platform.
A platform and community that provides tools for building, training, and deploying ML models.
An AI-powered search engine that provides direct answers with citations.
A European AI company focused on high-performance open and efficient models.
Elon Musk's AI company, developer of the Grok models.
An enterprise AI platform focusing on LLMs for business applications and RAG.
A data and AI company that created the DBRX model and the Spark platform.
A data cloud company that expanded into AI with the Arctic model and Cortex service.
The company behind Stable Diffusion and other open-source generative AI models.
An independent research lab exploring new mediums of thought and expanding imaginative powers of the human species.
A data infrastructure company that provides high-quality training data for AI models.
The company behind the popular LangChain framework and LangSmith platform.
A vector database company providing essential infrastructure for RAG and AI memory.
A developer-first MLOps platform for tracking and visualizing ML experiments.
A platform for interacting with AI characters using neural language models.
CEO of OpenAI and a prominent figure in the AI industry.
CEO and co-founder of Anthropic, formerly VP of Research at OpenAI.
CEO and co-founder of Google DeepMind, known for AlphaGo.
Chief AI Scientist at Meta and a pioneer in convolutional neural networks.
Co-founder of OpenAI, former Director of AI at Tesla, and prominent educator.
CTO of OpenAI, leading the development of GPT and DALL-E.
Co-founder and former Chief Scientist of OpenAI, key figure in Deep Learning.
Co-founder of Coursera and Google Brain, and a leading AI educator.
Professor at Stanford and co-director of HAI, known for ImageNet.
Known as the 'Godfather of AI' for his work on backpropagation and deep learning.
A leading figure in deep learning and recipient of the Turing Award.
CEO of Cohere and co-author of the 'Attention Is All You Need' paper.
CEO and co-founder of Mistral AI.
CEO and co-founder of Hugging Face.
CEO of Microsoft AI and co-founder of DeepMind and Inflection AI.
Co-founder and Chief AGI Scientist at Google DeepMind.
Co-founder of Essential AI and lead author of the 'Attention Is All You Need' paper.
CEO of Character.ai and key contributor to the Transformer architecture.
